NKJV.1: Why do the nations rage, And the people plot a vain thing?
NKJV.2: The kings of the earth set themselves, And the rulers take counsel together, Against the LORD and against His Anointed, saying,
NKJV.3: “Let us break Their bonds in pieces And cast away Their cords from us.”
NKJV.4: He who sits in the heavens shall laugh; The Lord shall hold them in derision.
NKJV.5: Then He shall speak to them in His wrath, And distress them in His deep displeasure:
NKJV.6: “Yet I have set My King On My holy hill of Zion.”
NKJV.7: “I will declare the decree: The LORD has said to Me, ‘You are My Son, Today I have begotten You.
NKJV.8: Ask of Me, and I will give You The nations for Your inheritance, And the ends of the earth for Your possession.
NKJV.9: You shall break them with a rod of iron; You shall dash them to pieces like a potter’s vessel.’ ”
NKJV.10: Now therefore, be wise, O kings; Be instructed, you judges of the earth.
NKJV.11: Serve the LORD with fear, And rejoice with trembling.
NKJV.12: Kiss the Son, lest He be angry, And you perish in the way, When His wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all those who put their trust in Him.

KJV.1: Why do the heathen<S>1471</S> rage<S>7283</S>, and the people<S>3816</S> imagine<S>1897</S> a vain thing<S>7385</S>? <sup>rage: or, tumultuously assemble</sup> <sup>imagine: Heb. meditate</sup>

KJV.5: Then shall he speak<S>1696</S> unto them in his wrath<S>639</S>, and vex<S>926</S> them in his sore displeasure<S>2740</S>. <sup>vex: or, trouble</sup>
KJV.6: Yet have I set<S>5258</S> my king<S>4428</S> upon my holy<S>6944</S> hill<S>2022</S> of Zion<S>6726</S>. <sup>set: Heb. anointed</sup> <sup>upon: Heb. upon Zion, the hill of my holiness</sup>
KJV.7: I will declare<S>5608</S> the decree<S>2706</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> hath said<S>559</S> unto me, Thou art my Son<S>1121</S>; this day<S>3117</S> have I begotten<S>3205</S> thee. <sup>the decree: or, for a decree</sup>
KJV.8: Ask<S>7592</S> of me, and I shall give<S>5414</S> thee the heathen<S>1471</S> for thine inheritance<S>5159</S>, and the uttermost parts<S>657</S> of the earth<S>776</S> for thy possession<S>272</S>.
KJV.9: Thou shalt break<S>7489</S> them with a rod<S>7626</S> of iron<S>1270</S>; thou shalt dash them in pieces<S>5310</S> like a potter's<S>3335</S> vessel<S>3627</S>.

ESV.1: Why do the nations rage  and the peoples plot in vain?
ESV.2: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD and against his Anointed, saying,
ESV.3: “Let us burst their bonds apart and cast away their cords from us.”
ESV.4: He who sits in the heavens laughs; the Lord holds them in derision.
ESV.5: Then he will speak to them in his wrath, and terrify them in his fury, saying,
ESV.6: “As for me, I have set my King on Zion, my holy hill.”
ESV.7: I will tell of the decree: The LORD said to me, “You are my Son; today I have begotten you.
ESV.8: Ask of me, and I will make the nations your heritage, and the ends of the earth your possession.
ESV.9: You shall break  them with a rod of iron and dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel.”
ESV.10: Now therefore, O kings, be wise; be warned, O rulers of the earth.
ESV.11: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
ESV.12: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and you perish in the way, for his wrath is quickly kindled. Blessed are all who take refuge in him.

NASB.1: Why are the nations in an uproar And the peoples devising a vain thing?
NASB.2: The kings of the earth take their stand And the rulers take counsel together Against the LORD and against His Anointed, saying,
NASB.3: 'Let us tear their fetters apart And cast away their cords from us!'
NASB.4: He who sits in the heavens laughs, The Lord scoffs at them.
NASB.5: Then He will speak to them in His anger And terrify them in His fury, saying,
NASB.6: 'But as for Me, I have installed My King Upon Zion, My holy mountain.'
NASB.7: 'I will surely tell of the decree of the LORD: He said to Me, 'You are My Son, Today I have begotten You.
NASB.8: 'Ask of Me, and I will surely give the nations as Your inheritance, And the [very] ends of the earth as Your possession.
NASB.9: 'You shall break them with a rod of iron, You shall shatter them like earthenware.''
NASB.10: Now therefore, O kings, show discernment; Take warning, O judges of the earth.
NASB.11: Worship the LORD with reverence And rejoice with trembling.
NASB.12: Do homage to the Son, that He not become angry, and you perish [in] the way, For His wrath may soon be kindled. How blessed are all who take refuge in Him!

NLT.1:
Why are the nations so angry?
Why do they waste their time with futile plans?
NLT.2:
The kings of the earth prepare for battle;
the rulers plot together
against the LORD
and against his anointed one.
NLT.3:
“Let us break their chains,” they cry,
“and free ourselves from slavery to God.”
NLT.4:
But the one who rules in heaven laughs.
The Lord scoffs at them.
NLT.5:
Then in anger he rebukes them,
terrifying them with his fierce fury.
NLT.6:
For the Lord declares, “I have placed my chosen king on the throne
in Jerusalem, on my holy mountain.”
NLT.7:
The king proclaims the LORD’s decree:
“The LORD said to me, ‘You are my son.
Today I have become your Father.
NLT.8:
Only ask, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance,
the whole earth as your possession.
NLT.9:
You will break them with an iron rod
and smash them like clay pots.’”
NLT.10:
Now then, you kings, act wisely!
Be warned, you rulers of the earth!
NLT.11:
Serve the LORD with reverent fear,
and rejoice with trembling.
NLT.12:
Submit to God’s royal son, or he will become angry,
and you will be destroyed in the midst of all your activities —
for his anger flares up in an instant.
But what joy for all who take refuge in him!

WEB.1: Why do the nations rage, and the peoples plot a vain thing?
WEB.2: The kings of the earth take a stand, and the rulers take counsel together, against Yahweh, and against his Anointed,  saying,
WEB.3: “Let’s break their bonds apart, and cast their cords from us.”
WEB.4: He who sits in the heavens will laugh. The Lord  will have them in derision.
WEB.5: Then he will speak to them in his anger, and terrify them in his wrath:
WEB.6: “Yet I have set my King on my holy hill of Zion.”
WEB.7: I will tell of the decree. Yahweh said to me, “You are my son. Today I have become your father.
WEB.8: Ask of me, and I will give the nations for your inheritance, the uttermost parts of the earth for your possession.
WEB.9: You shall break them with a rod of iron. You shall dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”
WEB.10: Now therefore be wise, you kings. Be instructed, you judges of the earth.
WEB.11: Serve Yahweh with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
WEB.12: Give sincere homage to the Son,  lest he be angry, and you perish on the way, for his wrath will soon be kindled. Blessed are all those who take refuge in him.

AMP.1:
Why are the nations in an uproar [in turmoil against God],
And why do the people devise a vain and hopeless plot?
AMP.2:
The kings of the earth take their stand;
And the rulers take counsel together
Against the LORD and His Anointed (the Davidic King, the Messiah, the Christ), saying,
AMP.3:
"Let us break apart their [divine] bands [of restraint]
And cast away their cords [of control] from us."
AMP.4:
He who sits [enthroned] in the heavens laughs [at their rebellion];
The [Sovereign] Lord scoffs at them [and in supreme contempt He mocks them].
AMP.5:
Then He will speak to them in His [profound] anger
And terrify them with His displeasure, saying,
AMP.6:
"Yet as for Me, I have anointed and firmly installed My King
Upon Zion, My holy mountain."
AMP.7:
"I will declare the decree of the LORD:
He said to Me, 'You are My Son;
This day [I proclaim] I have begotten You.
AMP.8:
'Ask of Me, and I will assuredly give [You] the nations as Your inheritance,
And the ends of the earth as Your possession.
AMP.9:
'You shall break them with a rod of iron;
You shall shatter them [in pieces] like earthenware.'"
AMP.10:
Now therefore, O kings, act wisely;
Be instructed and take warning, O leaders (judges, rulers) of the earth.
AMP.11:
Worship the LORD and serve Him with reverence [with awe-inspired fear and submissive wonder];
Rejoice [yet do so] with trembling.
AMP.12:
Kiss (pay respect to) the Son, so that He does not become angry, and you perish in the way,
For His wrath may soon be kindled and set aflame.
How blessed [fortunate, prosperous, and favored by God] are all those who take refuge in Him!

MSG.1:
Why the big noise, nations?
Why the mean plots, peoples?
MSG.2:
Earth-leaders push for position,
Demagogues and delegates meet for summit talks,
The God-deniers, the Messiah-defiers:
MSG.3:
"Let's get free of God!
Cast loose from Messiah!"
MSG.4:
Heaven-throned God breaks out laughing.
At first he's amused at their presumption;
MSG.5:
Then he gets good and angry.
Furiously, he shuts them up:
MSG.6:
"Don't you know there's a King in Zion? A coronation banquet
Is spread for him on the holy summit."
MSG.7:
Let me tell you what God said next.
He said, "You're my son,
And today is your birthday.
MSG.8:
What do you want? Name it:
Nations as a present? continents as a prize?
MSG.9:
You can command them all to dance for you,
Or throw them out with tomorrow's trash."
MSG.10:
So, rebel-kings, use your heads;
Upstart-judges, learn your lesson:
MSG.11:
Worship God in adoring embrace,
Celebrate in trembling awe.
MSG.12:
Kiss Messiah!
Your very lives are in danger, you know;
His anger is about to explode,
But if you make a run for God—you won't regret it!

NIV.1:
Psalm 2
Why do the nations conspire
and the peoples plot in vain?
NIV.2:
The kings of the earth take their stand
and the rulers gather together
against the Lord
and against his Anointed One.
NIV.3:
“Let us break their chains,” they say,
“and throw off their fetters.”
NIV.4:
The One enthroned in heaven laughs;
the Lord scoffs at them.
NIV.5:
Then he rebukes them in his anger
and terrifies them in his wrath, saying,
NIV.6:
“I have installed my King
on Zion, my holy hill.”
NIV.7:
I will proclaim the decree of the Lord:
He said to me, “You are my Son;
today I have become your Father.
NIV.8:
Ask of me,
and I will make the nations your inheritance,
the ends of the earth your possession.
NIV.9:
You will rule them with an iron scepter;
you will dash them to pieces like pottery.”
NIV.10:
Therefore, you kings, be wise;
be warned, you rulers of the earth.
NIV.11:
Serve the Lord with fear
and rejoice with trembling.
NIV.12:
Kiss the Son, lest he be angry
and you be destroyed in your way,
for his wrath can flare up in a moment.
Blessed are all who take refuge in him.

NRSVCE.1:
Why do the nations conspire,
and the peoples plot in vain?
NRSVCE.2:
The kings of the earth set themselves,
and the rulers take counsel together,
against the LORD and his anointed, saying,
NRSVCE.3:
“Let us burst their bonds asunder,
and cast their cords from us.”
NRSVCE.4:
He who sits in the heavens laughs;
the LORD has them in derision.
NRSVCE.5:
Then he will speak to them in his wrath,
and terrify them in his fury, saying,
NRSVCE.6: “I have set my king on Zion, my holy hill.”
NRSVCE.7:
I will tell of the decree of the LORD:
He said to me, “You are my son;
today I have begotten you.
NRSVCE.8:
Ask of me, and I will make the nations your heritage,
and the ends of the earth your possession.
NRSVCE.9:
You shall break them with a rod of iron,
and dash them in pieces like a potter’s vessel.”
NRSVCE.10:
Now therefore, O kings, be wise;
be warned, O rulers of the earth.
NRSVCE.11:
Serve the LORD with fear,
with trembling
NRSVCE.12:
kiss his feet,
or he will be angry, and you will perish in the way;
for his wrath is quickly kindled.
Happy are all who take refuge in him.

NET.1: Why do the nations rebel? Why are the countries devising plots that will fail?
NET.2: The kings of the earth form a united front; the rulers collaborate against the LORD and his anointed king.
NET.3: They say, “Let’s tear off the shackles they’ve put on us! Let’s free ourselves from their ropes!”
NET.4: The one enthroned in heaven laughs in disgust; the Lord taunts them.
NET.5: Then he angrily speaks to them and terrifies them in his rage, saying,
NET.6: “I myself have installed my king on Zion, my holy hill.”
NET.7: The king says, “I will announce the LORD’S decree. He said to me: ‘You are my son! This very day I have become your father!
NET.8: Ask me, and I will give you the nations as your inheritance, the ends of the earth as your personal property.
NET.9: You will break them with an iron scepter; you will smash them like a potter’s jar!’”
NET.10: So now, you kings, do what is wise; you rulers of the earth, submit to correction!
NET.11: Serve the LORD in fear! Repent in terror!
NET.12: Give sincere homage! Otherwise he will be angry, and you will die because of your behavior, when his anger quickly ignites. How blessed are all who take shelter in him!
